name: Tofu Steaks
recipeIngredient: cayenne and/or black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
(adapted from a recipe in the New Laurel's Kitchen) It makes great sandwiches.
1. Cut tofu into 1/4 inch thick pieces about the size of the bread you want to serve it on (I got 12 slices from a pound of tofu).
2. Combine the tomato paste, soy sauce, vinegar, mustard, and stock in a small bowl.
3. Heat the stock or water to boiling in a shallow skillet.
4. Saute the onion, garlic, and celery until just tender.
5. Add the sauteed vegetables to the sauce mixture and bring to a boil.
6. Simmer for 5 minutes.
7. Add tofu slices, making sure they are covered with sauce (try to get them in a single layer with only slight overlaps if possible).
8. Cook uncovered for about 20 minutes until most of the sauce has cooked away.
You can also cook this partially covered for 20 minutes and serve it over rice. I combined the methods by cooking it covered for 15 minutes, scooping some out to have with rice, then cooking the remaining stuff uncovered until the sauce was gone. This is a good dish, but I think next time I'll make it more of a barbecue sauce by adding some molasses and ketchup.
